entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop affected by Fusarium wilt.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A disease in chickpea caused by the fungal pathogen Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ciceris.

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. CICERIS
entities.description: A major fungal pathogen that causes Fusarium wilt in chickpea.

entities.name: LOOP-MEDIATED ISOTHERMAL AMPLIFICATION
entities.description: A rapid and inexpensive isothermal nucleic acid amplification technique used for the diagnosis of Fusarium wilt.

entities.name: EF1Á
entities.description: A gene sequence targeted by the LAMP assay for the detection of Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ciceris.

entities.name: HYDROXYNAPHTHOL BLUE DYE
entities.description: A dye used in the LAMP assay to provide a visual color change for positive reactions.

entities.name: AGAROSE GEL
entities.description: A medium used for electrophoresis to visualize ladder-like bands of LAMP products.

entities.name: ECONOMIC LOSSES
entities.description: The financial impact resulting from Fusarium wilt infections in chickpea fields.

entities.name: SOIL BORNE PATHOGENS
entities.description: Pathogens in the soil that produce symptoms similar to Fusarium wilt in chickpea.
